duration: 36 minutes-I left and came back, so the above times have more than 36 minutes between them.
description: The bird was not calling. It was more active and moved around more than the Canada Geese it was associating with. I initially thought it was the Pale-bellied/Atlantic version (hrota), but it seems that more experienced observers think it is Gray-bellied (see COBIRDS discussion here: https://groups.google.com/forum/#!topic/cobirds/-8D1VB_ZH_U ). As you can see in the attached photos, the bird was smaller than the Canadas, has a dark neck with a broken white collar. The legs are black. The belly is pale, as opposed to the darker belly that would be present on a black Brant. Please see the attached photos for better documentation.
